data.narrative:
NODA AI is a Series A defense and security software company founded by Philong Duong and Dave Corbett, currently carrying a $125M post-money valuation on $29M total raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 public peers across the defense and security sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling applies a standard 2.0x Series A-to-next-stage step-up to the $125M last post-money, yielding $250M. Peer-median benchmarking across three same-stage, same-sector comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—produces a $300M implied value. The peer range spans $150M to $500M.

These methods triangulate to a central valuation range of $212M to $345M, with a median of $275M. The $275M median sits 2.2x the current $125M mark, consistent with Series A expansion dynamics in frontier defense tech. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peers, customer concentration in government contracts, and the sustainability of near-term cash deployment. The $125M current valuation appears conservative relative to peer medians, though the stage-progression method suggests meaningful but not aggressive upside.
